name: Mexican Flank Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound meat on both sides with a meat mallet; sprinkle with salt, garlic salt, and pepper.
2. Unwrap tamales; place them in a bowl. Break up the tamales slightly with a fork and spread over the steaks.
3. Roll each steak into a jelly roll style, starting with the short side. Tie or skewer securely.
4. Place the meat rolls in a crockery cooker. Dissolve the bouillon in hot water, then combine with tomato sauce and Dijon mustard. Pour over the meat.
5. Cover and cook on low heat setting for 8 to 10 hours.
6. Lift out the meat rolls; remove strings or skewers. Pour cooking liquid into a saucepan and skim off excess fat.
7. Blend cold water into cornstarch and stir into the liquid. Cook until thickened and bubbly.
8. Spoon the sauce over the meat and sprinkle cheese atop each roll.
